notes: Passive, generic utility - iterates over every element with a 'class' attribute and removes any class that is exactly 'blur' or starts with 'blur-' (common CSS-blur-overlay class naming used by sites to obscure data). It does not show a modal, make network requests, or read any page-specific data, so it works on any page regardless of whether blurred elements are present. Wikipedia's Gaussian Blur article is a stable, generic public page; the bookmarklet should simply run without errors (whether or not any 'blur'/'blur-*' classes exist to remove). We check that images on the page are still present afterward as a basic sanity check that the page wasn't broken.

checks.comment: Page should still have images present after running the bookmarklet
